preview_text:
The model rates Brazil as the clear favorite, assigning them a 63% chance of victory against a Scotland side given only 12% to win. Scotland enters as a substantial underdog despite the neutral venue, with Brazil holding an approximate 50-percentage-point edge in win probability.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome at 25%, suggesting the match carries real competitive uncertainty beyond a binary Brazil win. The 12-point gap between Scotland and draw probability indicates Scotland's path to victory is narrower than a stalemate scenario, yet the overall spread—with Brazil's win probability in the low 60s—points toward a contest that is far from predetermined.

The market consensus slightly favors Brazil relative to the model, offering them 66% compared to the model's 63%. The sportsbook widens the gap to Scotland (14% vs 11.8%) and narrows the draw (20% vs 25%), a divergence of roughly 5 percentage points on the draw outcome. Polymarket prices trend even further toward Brazil. These differences are modest but worth noting; the dashboard presents the model's view without claiming superiority over consensus pricing.
